title: Piquant Turkish Eggplant Dolma
description: A chewy, low‑carb Turkish dish featuring spiced eggplant stuffed with cauliflower rice and lean lamb, served in a stone‑pot for a smoky finish.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 190°C and soak a stone pot in warm water for 10 minutes.
Cut a slit into the top of each eggplant and carefully scoop out the flesh to create a hollow shell, reserving the flesh for the filling.
In a skillet, cook the ground lamb over medium heat until browned, then add the onion and garlic and sauté until translucent.
Stir in the reserved eggplant flesh, cauliflower rice, tomato paste, cumin, paprika, dried mint, salt, and pepper; cook for 5 minutes.
Remove the skillet from heat and fold in the lemon juice and chopped parsley.
Fill each eggplant shell with the mixture, pressing gently to pack the stuffing.
Arrange the stuffed eggplants upright in the preheated stone pot.
Pour 1 cup of water around the eggplants, ensuring the water covers the base of the pot but not the tops of the eggplants.
Place the pot on a low‑heat burner until the water simmers, then cover and continue cooking for 30 minutes.
After cooking, remove the pot from heat and allow the dolmas to cool slightly before serving.
Serve the dolmas hot, spooning some of the cooking liquid over each eggplant for extra moisture.
Optionally garnish with a sprinkle of fresh mint or parsley before plating.
